criteria.name: Story Angle Selection
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: Chooses a reportable angle based on news value, verification, deadline fit, and reader meaning instead of rumor. Score only this task; do not use later notes, prep work, or downstream artifacts as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Reporting Plan
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: The plan gives specific Coach Harris and Reed questions, identifies a confirmed fact and source, and names one unconfirmed claim to leave out with a clear reason. Score only this planning task; do not use later notes, interviews, or article work as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Warmup Observation
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: Separates official information, observed movement, rumor, and medical claims, and clearly distinguishes publishable facts from material to hold or verify. Score only this observation task; do not use later prep notes or artifacts as evidence. If a safety-critical source distinction is skipped, do not award full credit even if other elements are strong. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Coach Harris Questioning
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: In the coach availability transcript, asks short, specific coach-appropriate questions and avoids asking Coach Harris to answer for Reed personally or confirm unsupported claims. Score only this interview task; do not use later notes or article work as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Player Scrum Questioning
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: In the player scrum transcript, asks player-friendly, answerable questions that can produce attributable story material without pressure, leading wording, or unsupported health claims. Score only this interview task; do not use later article work as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Game Observation Notes
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: Organizes late-game events, observations, stats, usable scenes, and hold-back items in a form that supports a deadline game story. Score only this possession timeline task; do not use later interview or article work as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.

criteria.name: Deadline Game Article
criteria.rubric_text: 9-10: Uses only confirmed facts and interview material, selects safe and strong paragraphs for News Lead, Proof, Angle, and Close, and writes a clear deadline headline. Score only this article assembly task; do not use unrelated notes as evidence. 7-8: The main elements are present, but one small gap in evidence, specificity, priority, notes, or response quality remains. 4-6: Some work is useful, but multiple important decisions, checks, explanations, notes, or next actions are missing. 0-3: The response misses the task goal, ignores evidence, creates risky claims or confusion, or is not usable.
